channel.title: Xen Project — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Xen Project (xen).

item.title: CVE-2026-42486 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Multiple RBAC issues in XAPI
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] XAPI can configure different users with different roles, using Role Based Access Control. For more details, see: https://…

item.title: CVE-2026-23562 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Multiple RBAC issues in XAPI
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] XAPI can configure different users with different roles, using Role Based Access Control. For more details, see: https://…

item.title: CVE-2026-23561 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Multiple RBAC issues in XAPI
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] XAPI can configure different users with different roles, using Role Based Access Control. For more details, see: https://…

item.title: CVE-2026-23560 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Multiple RBAC issues in XAPI
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] XAPI can configure different users with different roles, using Role Based Access Control. For more details, see: https://…

item.title: CVE-2026-23559 · 9.4 CRITICAL — Multiple RBAC issues in XAPI
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] XAPI can configure different users with different roles, using Role Based Access Control. For more details, see: https://…

item.title: CVE-2026-23556 · 9.4 CRITICAL — oxenstored keeps quota related use counts across domain destruction
item.description: When oxenstored is tearing a domain down, the node data is cleaned up but the usage counts are leaked. When the domain ID is eventually reused, the new domain can create fewer nodes before beeing deemed to be over quota.

item.title: CVE-2025-58151 · 9.4 CRITICAL — varstored: TOCTOU issues with mapped guest memory
item.description: varstored is a component of the Xapi toolstack handling UEFI Variables for a VM. It has a communication path with OVMF inside the VM involving mapping a buffer prepared by OVMF. Within varstored, there were insufficient compiler barriers, creating TO…

item.title: CVE-2025-58146 · 9.4 CRITICAL — XAPI UTF-8 string handling
item.description: There are multiple issues. 1. Updates to the XAPI database sanitise input strings, but try generating the notification using the unsanitised input. This causes the database's event thread to terminate and cease further processing. 2. XAPI's UTF-8 enc…

item.title: CVE-2025-27464 · 9.4 CRITICAL — WinPVDrivers: Excessive permissions on user-exposed devices
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] The Windows PV drivers expose various facilities to userspace. Several of these have no security descriptor, and are ther…

item.title: CVE-2025-27463 · 9.4 CRITICAL — WinPVDrivers: Excessive permissions on user-exposed devices
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] The Windows PV drivers expose various facilities to userspace. Several of these have no security descriptor, and are ther…

item.title: CVE-2025-27462 · 9.4 CRITICAL — WinPVDrivers: Excessive permissions on user-exposed devices
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] The Windows PV drivers expose various facilities to userspace. Several of these have no security descriptor, and are ther…

item.title: CVE-2026-42488 — x86: mismatched mapcache metadata
item.description: Some shadow paging errors paths will switch the page-tables without updating the currently running vCPU reference. This causes a mismatch between the loaded page-tables and the mapcache metadata which can lead to corruption of the mapcache.

item.title: CVE-2026-42490 — domctl lock open to abuse
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] To create and manage guests, domctl operations are used by the control domain, a possible Xenstore domain, or by a domain…

item.title: CVE-2026-42489 — domctl lock open to abuse
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] To create and manage guests, domctl operations are used by the control domain, a possible Xenstore domain, or by a domain…

item.title: CVE-2026-42487 — x86 HVM I/O port list traversal
item.description: HVM guest I/O port accesses are subject to either emulation or at least translation. Translations are managed by the device model (via XEN_DOMCTL_ioport_mapping), and hence the linked list used may changed at any time. Traversal of those lists (while…

item.title: CVE-2026-23558 · 7.8 HIGH — grant table v2 race in status page mapping
item.description: The adjustments made for XSA-379 as well as those subsequently becoming XSA-387 still left a race window, when a HVM or PVH guest does a grant table version change from v2 to v1 in parallel with mapping the status page(s) via XENMEM_add_to_physmap. S…

item.title: CVE-2026-23557 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Xenstored DoS via XS_RESET_WATCHES command
item.description: Any guest can cause xenstored to crash by issuing a XS_RESET_WATCHES command within a transaction due to an assert() triggering. In case xenstored was built with NDEBUG #defined nothing bad will happen, as assert() is doing nothing in this case. Note…

item.title: CVE-2026-23555 · 7.1 HIGH — Xenstored DoS by unprivileged domain
item.description: Any guest issuing a Xenstore command accessing a node using the (illegal) node path "/local/domain/", will crash xenstored due to a clobbered error indicator in xenstored when verifying the node path. Note that the crash is forced via a failing asser…

item.title: CVE-2026-23554 · 7.8 HIGH — Use after free of paging structures in EPT
item.description: The Intel EPT paging code uses an optimization to defer flushing of any cached EPT state until the p2m lock is dropped, so that multiple modifications done under the same locked region only issue a single flush. Freeing of paging structures however i…

item.title: CVE-2026-23553 · 2.9 LOW — x86: incomplete IBPB for vCPU isolation
item.description: In the context switch logic Xen attempts to skip an IBPB in the case of a vCPU returning to a CPU on which it was the previous vCPU to run. While safe for Xen's isolation between vCPUs, this prevents the guest kernel correctly isolating between tasks…

item.title: CVE-2025-58150 · 8.8 HIGH — x86: buffer overrun with shadow paging + tracing
item.description: Shadow mode tracing code uses a set of per-CPU variables to avoid cumbersome parameter passing. Some of these variables are written to with guest controlled data, of guest controllable size. That size can be larger than the variable, and bounding of…

item.title: CVE-2025-58149 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Incorrect removal of permissions on PCI device unplug
item.description: When passing through PCI devices, the detach logic in libxl won't remove access permissions to any 64bit memory BARs the device might have. As a result a domain can still have access any 64bit memory BAR when such device is no longer assigned to the…

item.title: CVE-2025-58148 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — x86: Incorrect input sanitisation in Viridian hypercalls
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] Some Viridian hypercalls can specify a mask of vCPU IDs as an input, in one of three formats. Xen has boundary checking b…

item.title: CVE-2025-58147 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — x86: Incorrect input sanitisation in Viridian hypercalls
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] Some Viridian hypercalls can specify a mask of vCPU IDs as an input, in one of three formats. Xen has boundary checking b…

item.title: CVE-2025-58145 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Arm issues with page refcounting
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] There are two issues related to the mapping of pages belonging to other domains: For one, an assertion is wrong there, wh…

item.title: CVE-2025-58144 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Arm issues with page refcounting
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] There are two issues related to the mapping of pages belonging to other domains: For one, an assertion is wrong there, wh…

item.title: CVE-2025-58143 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Mutiple vulnerabilities in the Viridian interface
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] There are multiple issues related to the handling and accessing of guest memory pages in the viridian code: 1. A NULL poi…

item.title: CVE-2025-58142 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Mutiple vulnerabilities in the Viridian interface
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] There are multiple issues related to the handling and accessing of guest memory pages in the viridian code: 1. A NULL poi…

item.title: CVE-2025-27466 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Mutiple vulnerabilities in the Viridian interface
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] There are multiple issues related to the handling and accessing of guest memory pages in the viridian code: 1. A NULL poi…

item.title: CVE-2025-1713 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — deadlock potential with VT-d and legacy PCI device pass-through
item.description: When setting up interrupt remapping for legacy PCI(-X) devices, including PCI(-X) bridges, a lookup of the upstream bridge is required. This lookup, itself involving acquiring of a lock, is done in a context where acquiring that lock is unsafe. This…

item.title: CVE-2025-27465 · 4.3 MEDIUM — x86: Incorrect stubs exception handling for flags recovery
item.description: Certain instructions need intercepting and emulating by Xen. In some cases Xen emulates the instruction by replaying it, using an executable stub. Some instructions may raise an exception, which is supposed to be handled gracefully. Certain replayed…

item.title: CVE-2024-31144 · 3.8 LOW — Xapi: Metadata injection attack against backup/restore functionality
item.description: For a brief summary of Xapi terminology, see: https://xapi-project.github.io/xen-api/overview.html#object-model-overview Xapi contains functionality to backup and restore metadata about Virtual Machines and Storage Repositories (SRs). The metadata it…

item.title: CVE-2024-45819 · 5.5 MEDIUM — libxl leaks data to PVH guests via ACPI tables
item.description: PVH guests have their ACPI tables constructed by the toolstack. The construction involves building the tables in local memory, which are then copied into guest memory. While actually used parts of the local memory are filled in correctly, excess spac…

item.title: CVE-2024-45818 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Deadlock in x86 HVM standard VGA handling
item.description: The hypervisor contains code to accelerate VGA memory accesses for HVM guests, when the (virtual) VGA is in "standard" mode. Locking involved there has an unusual discipline, leaving a lock acquired past the return from the function that acquired it.…

item.title: CVE-2024-45817 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — x86: Deadlock in vlapic_error()
item.description: In x86's APIC (Advanced Programmable Interrupt Controller) architecture, error conditions are reported in a status register. Furthermore, the OS can opt to receive an interrupt when a new error occurs. It is possible to configure the error interrupt…

item.title: CVE-2024-31146 · 7.5 HIGH — PCI device pass-through with shared resources
item.description: When multiple devices share resources and one of them is to be passed through to a guest, security of the entire system and of respective guests individually cannot really be guaranteed without knowing internals of any of the involved guests. Therefo…

item.title: CVE-2024-31145 · 7.5 HIGH — error handling in x86 IOMMU identity mapping
item.description: Certain PCI devices in a system might be assigned Reserved Memory Regions (specified via Reserved Memory Region Reporting, "RMRR") for Intel VT-d or Unity Mapping ranges for AMD-Vi. These are typically used for platform tasks such as legacy USB emula…

item.title: CVE-2024-31143 · 7.5 HIGH — double unlock in x86 guest IRQ handling
item.description: An optional feature of PCI MSI called "Multiple Message" allows a device to use multiple consecutive interrupt vectors. Unlike for MSI-X, the setting up of these consecutive vectors needs to happen all in one go. In this handling an error path could…

item.title: CVE-2024-31142 · 7.5 HIGH — x86: Incorrect logic for BTC/SRSO mitigations
item.description: Because of a logical error in XSA-407 (Branch Type Confusion), the mitigation is not applied properly when it is intended to be used. XSA-434 (Speculative Return Stack Overflow) uses the same infrastructure, so is equally impacted. For more details,…

item.title: CVE-2023-46842 · 6.5 MEDIUM — x86 HVM hypercalls may trigger Xen bug check
item.description: Unlike 32-bit PV guests, HVM guests may switch freely between 64-bit and other modes. This in particular means that they may set registers used to pass 32-bit-mode hypercall arguments to values outside of the range 32-bit code would be able to set th…

item.title: CVE-2023-46841 · 6.5 MEDIUM — x86: shadow stack vs exceptions from emulation stubs
item.description: Recent x86 CPUs offer functionality named Control-flow Enforcement Technology (CET). A sub-feature of this are Shadow Stacks (CET-SS). CET-SS is a hardware feature designed to protect against Return Oriented Programming attacks. When enabled, traditi…

item.title: CVE-2023-46840 · 4.1 MEDIUM — VT-d: Failure to quarantine devices in !HVM builds
item.description: Incorrect placement of a preprocessor directive in source code results in logic that doesn't operate as intended when support for HVM guests is compiled out of Xen.

item.title: CVE-2023-46839 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — pci: phantom functions assigned to incorrect contexts
item.description: PCI devices can make use of a functionality called phantom functions, that when enabled allows the device to generate requests using the IDs of functions that are otherwise unpopulated. This allows a device to extend the number of outstanding request…

item.title: CVE-2023-46838 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Linux: netback processing of zero-length transmit fragment
item.description: Transmit requests in Xen's virtual network protocol can consist of multiple parts. While not really useful, except for the initial part any of them may be of zero length, i.e. carry no data at all. Besides a certain initial portion of the to be trans…

item.title: CVE-2023-46837 · 3.3 LOW — arm32: The cache may not be properly cleaned/invalidated (take two)
item.description: Arm provides multiple helpers to clean & invalidate the cache for a given region. This is, for instance, used when allocating guest memory to ensure any writes (such as the ones during scrubbing) have reached memory before handing over the page to a…

item.title: CVE-2023-46836 · 4.7 MEDIUM — x86: BTC/SRSO fixes not fully effective
item.description: The fixes for XSA-422 (Branch Type Confusion) and XSA-434 (Speculative Return Stack Overflow) are not IRQ-safe. It was believed that the mitigations always operated in contexts with IRQs disabled. However, the original XSA-254 fix for Meltdown (XPTI)…

item.title: CVE-2023-46835 · 5.5 MEDIUM — x86/AMD: mismatch in IOMMU quarantine page table levels
item.description: The current setup of the quarantine page tables assumes that the quarantine domain (dom_io) has been initialized with an address width of DEFAULT_DOMAIN_ADDRESS_WIDTH (48) and hence 4 page table levels. However dom_io being a PV domain gets the AMD-V…

item.title: CVE-2023-34328 · 5.5 MEDIUM — x86/AMD: Debug Mask handling
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] AMD CPUs since ~2014 have extensions to normal x86 debugging functionality. Xen supports guests using these extensions. U…

item.title: CVE-2023-34327 · 5.5 MEDIUM — x86/AMD: Debug Mask handling
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] AMD CPUs since ~2014 have extensions to normal x86 debugging functionality. Xen supports guests using these extensions. U…

item.title: CVE-2023-34325 · 7.8 HIGH — Multiple vulnerabilities in libfsimage disk handling
item.description: [This CNA information record relates to multiple CVEs; the text explains which aspects/vulnerabilities correspond to which CVE.] libfsimage contains parsing code for several filesystems, most of them based on grub-legacy code. libfsimage is used by p…
